sections.paragraph:
not used in Kal, to set up (ftellen), like the Syr. ܨܘܐܳ (see Eze 30:15, Pesh.), whence ܨܘܳܝܐܳ, Arab. صُوَّةُ cippus, Hebr. צִיוּן.

Piel צִוָּה fut. apoc. וַיְצַו imp. apoc. צַו, Arab. وَصَّى-

(1) to constitute, to appoint (beftellen)-(a) any one over any thing, followed by an acc. of person, and עַל of the thing, 1Sa 13:14 25:30 2Sa 6:21 Neh 5:14 7:2 .-(b) with an acc. of the thing, to appoint, to decree, to determine any thing; i.e. to cause it to exist. Isa 45:12, “all the host of it (the heaven) have I appointed,” caused to exist; 48:5 Deu 28:8 Psa 68:29 111:9 133:3 .

(2) to charge, to command, followed by acc. of pers. (like the Lat. jussit aliquem), Gen 26:11 more rarely followed by עַל 2:16 28:6 Est 2:10, 20 followed by אֶל Gen 50:16 followed by לְ Exo 1:22. The express words of the command are subjoined with לֵאמֹר e.g. Gen 26:11, וַיְצַו אֲבִימֶלֶךְ אֶת־בָּל־הָעָם לֵאמֹר “and Abimelech commanded all the people, saying,” etc.; 32:5 Exo 5:6. In other places, that which any one is commanded to do, is put in the inf. with לְ prefixed, Gen 50:2 2Sa 7:7 Jer 35:8 more rarely in a finite verb, with אֲשֶׁר (that) prefixed, Est 2:10 and ו (prop. he charged, and he did), Amo 9:4. To command any one, any thing, with two acc. of pers. and thing, Gen 6:22 Exo 25:22. It is also used without any mention added of the command, with an acc. of pers. to give commands to any one (jem. etwas beftellen), to delegate any one with commands, to commission, Jerem. 14:14 23:32 followed by עַל of the person to whom the commands are sent, Est 4:5 Ezr 8:17 1Ch 22:12 or of the thing about which the command is given, Gen 12:20 2Sa 14:8 to which also אֶל is prefixed, Exo 6:13 and לְ Psa 91:11. Used of inanimate things, Amo 9:4 Psa 42:9. Specially it is said צִוָּה בֵיתֹו or צִוָּה לְבֵיתֹו to give (last) commands to one’s family, i.e. to make a will (fein Haus beftellen), 2Sa 17:23 2Ki 20:1 Isa 38:1 (comp. Rabbin. צַוָּאָה testament).

Pual, to be commanded. Levit. 8:35 , בֵּן צֻוֵּיתִי “so am I commanded,” this charge is given to me. Eze 12:7, בַאֲשֶׁר צֻוֵּיתִי “even as I was commanded.” Followed by בְּ of the person who gives the command, Num 36:2.

Derivatives, צַו, צִיּוּן, מִצְוָה [“and צִי”].

צרה

(with Kametz impure) f. of the word צַר (from the root צָרַר)-

(1) a female adversary, enemy, especially a rival, 1Sa 1:6 (from the root צָרַר No. 4).

(2) distress, with ה parag. Psa 120:1, בַּצָּרָתָה לִי “when I am in distress,” comp. Jon 2:3 from the root צָרַר No. 5.
